pr.title: Misc all star / adventure mode matches
pr.author: r-burns

summary: Matched and typed several single-player game-mode routines around All-Star, Adventure, Classic game-over/debug-game-over data flow, and All-Star CSS setup. The PR moved DebugGameOverData into shared gm/types.h, added forward typedefs, refined UnkAllstarData and gmm main-lib substructures, replaced several UNK_T/UNK_PARAMS prototypes with typed signatures, and implemented previously stubbed routines such as gm_8017C9A8, gm_801B4254, gm_801B4294, gm_801B5E7C, gm_801B5EB4, gm_801B5EE4, gm_801B5F50, gm_801B5FB4, and gm_801B62D8_OnInit.
what_changed: Added shared forward typedefs for DebugGameOverData and UnkAllstarData in gm/forward.h, replacing scattered `struct UnkAllstarData*` spelling in multiple files with the shared typedef style.
Moved `struct DebugGameOverData` out of gm_1B03.static.h into gm/types.h and adjusted its layout to include `int xC` instead of padding at 0xC, enabling typed use by game-over minor scene code.

Recovered part of `struct gmm_x0` at offset 0x528 as nested `struct gmm_x0_528_t` with fields `c_kind`, `stocks`, `color`, `cpu_level`, `x4`, and `x5`; updated `gmMainLib_8015CDE0` to return `struct gmm_x0_528_t*`.

Refined `struct UnkAllstarData` field meanings and layout, documenting early fields as character kind, color, CPU level, slot, and stocks; changed nested `xC.xC` from byte padding to an `int`; added `u16 x74` before the remaining padding.
Implemented `gm_8017C9A8(DebugGameOverData*, UnkAllstarData*, u8)`, filling debug game-over data from All-Star data, `gm_801623D8()`, and the external `lbl_803B7C08[][5]` lookup.

Typed several gm_17C0 interfaces: `gm_8017C9A8`, `gm_8017CA38`, `gm_8017D7AC`, `fn_8017DEC8`, `fn_8017DF28`, `gm_8017E424`, and `gm_8017EB30` now use `DebugGameOverData*`, `MatchExitInfo*`, and `UnkAllstarData*` where known.

Implemented Adventure minor-scene bridge routines `gm_801B4254` and `gm_801B4294`, using `gm_801A427C`/`gm_801A4284` to access scene data and forwarding through `gm_8017C9A8`/`gm_8017CA38` with Adventure's `gm_8017E424()` All-Star-data source and mode index 0.

Implemented several All-Star routines: `gm_801B5E7C`, `gm_801B5EB4`, `gm_801B5EE4`, `gm_801B5F50`, `gm_801B5FB4`, and `gm_801B62D8_OnInit`, covering match-exit processing, game-over setup, CSS data initialization/update, pending minor-scene handling, and reset of `gmm_x0_528_t` defaults.
Updated Classic and All-Star minor scene tables to pass `&gm_80470850` now that `gm_80470850` is typed as a single `DebugGameOverData` object rather than an `UNK_T[]` array.
Improved external globals in several files, including `gm_80470850` as `DebugGameOverData`, `gm_80495370` as `MatchExitInfo`, and `gm_80495238` as `VsModeData`, reducing untyped array declarations.
Removed redundant extern declarations for several globals from gm_1B03.c, presumably because declarations are now available through headers or no longer needed locally.
Refined `gm_801B06B0` parameter names in gm_1B03.h to expose `match_type`, `c_kind`, `stocks`, `color`, `cpu_level`, and `slot`.
smart_moves: Centralizing `DebugGameOverData` in gm/types.h made the same data object usable from Classic, Adventure, All-Star, and gm_17C0 without duplicating a static-only struct definition.
Typing `gmMainLib_8015CDE0` as `struct gmm_x0_528_t*` unlocked clean field-based code in All-Star CSS setup instead of pointer arithmetic or opaque `void*` access.

The implementation of `gm_8017C9A8` used direct field transfers from `UnkAllstarData` into `DebugGameOverData`, plus the small `lbl_803B7C08[arg2][arg1->x2]` lookup, which is a compact match for the data-marshalling behavior.

Updating minor-scene table entries from `gm_80470850` to `&gm_80470850` preserved the existing scene-data semantics after changing the global from an array-like `UNK_T[]` declaration to a typed object.
The PR propagated type improvements through headers first, allowing multiple mode files to share stronger prototypes and reducing repeated `struct UnkAllstarData` forward declarations.

Using named fields like `c_kind`, `stocks`, `color`, `cpu_level`, and `slot` clarified the argument order for `gm_801B06B0`, which matters because many parameters are small integer types and easy to swap.
decomp_lessons: When a once-opaque global is really a singleton struct, update callers to take its address explicitly; this can resolve type correctness without changing the underlying data flow.
Small recovered substructures inside large mode-global blobs can make later matching substantially easier: recovering `gmm_x0_528_t` enabled natural assignments in All-Star setup routines.
Promoting structs from a static header to shared gm/types.h is useful when multiple game modes begin to pass the same data through common helper functions.

A function that only copies selected fields between structs is often the key to recovering both source and destination layouts; `gm_8017C9A8` provided evidence for offsets in both `DebugGameOverData` and `UnkAllstarData`.
Parameter names in prototypes are valuable decomp documentation when many arguments share types such as `u8` and `s8`; they also help future call-site recovery avoid argument-order mistakes.

The All-Star, Adventure, and Classic minor-scene code reuses common gm_17C0 helpers with different mode indices, so future single-player mode matching should check for shared marshalling helpers before duplicating logic.
Adding a typed accessor return, such as `gmMainLib_8015CDE0`, can be a low-risk way to replace several unknown fields with a meaningful local struct.
naming_conventions: Kept unknown/recovered-but-not-named structs under project-style placeholder names such as `UnkAllstarData` and `gmm_x0_528_t` while adding field comments for known meanings.
Used field comments like `///< c_kind`, `///< color`, `///< cpu_level`, `///< slot`, and `///< stocks` rather than prematurely renaming every field in `UnkAllstarData`.
Used typed global declarations matching the data kind, e.g. `DebugGameOverData gm_80470850`, `MatchExitInfo gm_80495370`, and `VsModeData gm_80495238`, instead of generic `UNK_T[]`.

Function prototypes retain address-based names such as `gm_8017C9A8` but now include semantically meaningful parameter types and some names, matching current doldecomp style for partially decompiled modules.
Nested struct name `gmm_x0_528_t` encodes its owner and offset, a common convention for partially recovered embedded structs.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Used `PAD_STACK(8)` in `gm_8017C9A8`, indicating stack-padding preservation was needed for matching.
Matched field-copy routines by preserving exact small integer types: `gm_8017C9A8` takes `u8 arg2`, writes it into `x8`, and indexes `lbl_803B7C08[arg2][arg1->x2]`.
Changed `gm_801623D8` from unknown params/return to `int gm_801623D8(void)`, enabling its result to be assigned directly to `DebugGameOverData.x18` in the matched implementation.

In All-Star CSS update code, used early return on `pending_scene_change == 2`, followed by `gm_801B0730`, field copies into `gm_80473A18`, `gm_SetScenePendingMinor((temp_r30->x5 * 8) & 0xF8)`, `gm_80168F88()`, and `gm_801B5324`, preserving a very specific control/data sequence.
Included `lbdvd.h` and called `lbDvd_800174BC()` in `gm_801B5F50`, suggesting the matched routine required the DVD/library side effect after preparing CSS data.
review_feedback: No review comments were present in the provided PR context.
No PR body or human text excerpt was provided, so intent beyond the title and diff is inferred only from changed code.
searchable_terms: Misc all star / adventure mode matches
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that rename `UnkAllstarData` or further document its fields, especially offsets x0-x5, nested xC, and x74.
Search for subsequent matches of `gm_8017CA38`, `gm_8017D7AC`, `gm_801B5ACC`, `gm_801B60A4_OnLoad`, and remaining stubs in gmallstar.c/gm_17C0.c.
Check whether `DebugGameOverData.xC` receives a meaningful name after callers test `data->xC != 0` in `gm_801B5EE4`.
Look for PRs that further type `gmMainLib_8015CDD4` similarly to `gmMainLib_8015CDE0`.
Search whether `lbl_803B7C08` is later typed or named as a character/CPU-level lookup table.
